sessions.intent: Set up the rename plan for Ingi to critique — take ownership from coder's v1 draft, fix bisection hygiene, define Phase 4 groupings, scope Builder in.

before.assumptions: Coder wrote a v1 plan + captured baseline; Ingi wants the architect to own the plan shape so he can critique it. The branch already has a settled goal (lowercase PLang vocabulary, keep C# infra PascalCase).
before.risk: Plan reads as a draft pulled from coder rather than a designed structure. Mixing semantic change (Modules merge) with cosmetic rename poisons bisection.

actions.context: Architect spine. Six phases: root, Data/Type, Path, vocabulary sweep (4a-4d grouped by blast radius), Builder, close. Modules merge pulled out per Ingi's call. Builder lowercased per Ingi's call. Phase 4 groupings defined by blast radius not coupling — execution core / I/O / state-lifecycle / control-diagnostics.

after.status: Plan written, awaiting Ingi's critique. No code changes yet.
after.notes: Coder's v1 plan + baseline remain in .bot/app-lowercase/coder/v1/ as reference. Once Ingi signs off, this plan replaces coder's as the source of truth.

sessions.intent: Fix all 'App.' string literals in test files and production code after the App → app namespace rename.

before.assumptions: Branch already has the namespace rename in production C#. Test files have hardcoded 'App.' strings in reflection calls, assertions, and Roslyn stub sources.
before.risk: Generator tests use inline Roslyn source that must use 'app.modules' for the generator to recognize [Action] attributes. Fixture DLLs need rebuilding.

actions.context: Changed 'App.' string literals to 'app.' in reflection GetType() calls, assertions, generated file names, and inline Roslyn compilation stubs.
Updated fixture source files using directives from App.* to app.*, rebuilt DLLs and committed them.

Fixed StoreOnlyModifier namespace check from 'App.Goals' to 'app.Goals' — caused SaveGoal_CamelCase_StoreOnly to fail because non-[Store] properties (Errors, Warnings) were no longer filtered out of .pr JSON.

after.status: All 2752 tests passing. Both the original 16 string-literal failures and a hidden production bug in StoreOnlyModifier are fixed.
after.notes: The StoreOnlyModifier bug was not in the original task description but was discovered during test runs. Fixed as part of this session.

sessions.intent: Phase 4 namespace rename: fix ~80 source files where type-position bare references use old PascalCase names after top-level app/ folders renamed to lowercase.

before.assumptions: Directories already renamed. ~1260 build errors in source files using old PascalCase namespace references in type positions.
before.risk: sed-based replacement may hit expression-position property accesses (app.Goals.X) which must stay PascalCase. Global aliases (Snapshot, Variables, Step, Channel, Serializers) make Alias.@this invalid — must use alias alone.

actions.context: Fixed all type-position references after top-level app/ folder rename to lowercase. Key rules: Alias.@this is invalid (use alias alone); expression-position property accesses stay PascalCase; app.callstack.call.Errors namespace stayed PascalCase (exception); global:: qualifier required when relative resolution fails.

after.status: Build clean: 0 errors, 46 warnings. PLang.csproj and PLangConsole.csproj both build successfully.
after.notes: Discovered: (1) Global alias Foo.@this is always wrong — alias IS the @this type. (2) sed replaced expression-position property accesses (app.Goals, app.FileSystem) which needed reverting. (3) app.callstack.call.Errors is PascalCase namespace (exception). (4) Variables.Navigators.ValueNavigators needed global:: path since Variables is a type alias not a namespace.

sessions.intent: Read-and-report pass on the app-lowercase rename sweep + 7 OBP merges before merge to runtime2.

before.assumptions: Coder reports 2752 C# tests + 203 PLang tests green at every commit boundary. Two breaking plang API renames (app.run->environment.run, builder.app->builder.load) flagged as placeholders. claude-md-proposals already filed.
before.risk: The OBP merges (7 commits) were scoped *out* of the rename branch by the architect plan but pulled in interactively. Merges are real shape changes — possible orphan files, duplicate state, or shallow consolidations that did not actually absorb the discipline.

actions.context: Read-only audit of app-lowercase branch: 5 passes per character file, full clean rebuild + both test suites. Verdict PASS with three low-severity follow-ups.
OBP shape verification on the 7 merged folders. All single-owner with private mutation discipline. Verified each resolved the prior case-pair smell-#3 without introducing new shape smells.
Pass-4 miss: app/data/Code/ stayed PascalCase while every other code-provider folder is lowercase. Flagged as finding S1 for coder follow-up.
Stale App.X docstring/comment references (~8 sites). No runtime impact. Flagged as finding R1/R2 for cleanup commit.

after.status: PASS. 2752/2752 C# tests + 203/6-known-fail PLang tests. Three low-severity follow-ups (S1 lowercase miss on app/data/Code/, R1 ~8 stale docstrings, S2 document Default carve-out) — none blocking the merge to runtime2.
after.notes: Branch ships two workstreams together (rename + 7 OBP merges). The merges resolved real prior smell-#3 violations and introduced no new shape smells. Branch is ready for tester.

sessions.intent: First tester pass on app-lowercase after merging origin/runtime2 (foundation-verify error.handle tests). Validate that the rename + the new tests compose, no regressions, and the 3 new .pr files are not builder false greens.

before.assumptions: Coder v1 baseline (pre-rename, on 199b4997) was 2752/2752 C# and 203 pass + 6 expected-fail fixtures plang. Merge brought in 3 new Errors/*RecoveryValue*.test.goal files. Codeanalyzer already PASSed on the rename shape.
before.risk: Reflection-driven action discovery could miss renamed namespaces silently. The 3 new tests could be builder false greens — action shifted under the same step text would still pass on assertions that don't pin enough. The 2026-04-27 recovery-value fix only matters if tests pin both the side-effect AND %!error% suppression.

actions.context: Reset staged harness-injected CLAUDE.md diff before switching branches — that diff is per-agent preamble that doesn't belong in the tracked repo file. The /CLAUDE.md proposal workflow exists for genuine canonical additions.

git merge origin/runtime2 --no-edit on app-lowercase. 5 commits, no conflicts. Merge commit f24356380. Brought in runtime2-foundation-verify error.handle recovery-value tests + todos.md reconciliation. Pushed to origin.

Clean rebuild from zero before running plang --test. Stale-binary trap rule from CLAUDE.md: phantom 'Action not found' errors on a renamed surface like app-lowercase are the exact pattern stale binaries produce; rebuild kills that risk.

Full C# + PLang test runs. C# 2752/2752 pass, identical to coder v1 baseline at 199b4997. PLang 206 real passes + 6 pre-existing expected-fail fixtures (delta +3 from baseline = the 3 new Errors/*RecoveryValue*.test.goal merged in).

Builder false-green check. Every step's text semantically matches actions[*].module.action. error.handle lands as a modifier on error.throw with the right Order / RetryCount / Actions parameters in each. No shift, no shadow.

Assertion quality check. Each test pins both side-effect (%content%/%first%/%second%) AND %!error% is null. The second is the symmetry pin for the 2026-04-27 handle.cs fix — without it an Ok()-without-Handled regression slips by; with it, deletion test catches the bug.
Tester deliverables for v1. Verdict: PASS, no findings.

after.health: Merge result is healthy. C# 2752/2752, PLang 206/206 real passes, 3 new tests are honestly green (no builder false greens, strong assertions). Pre-existing deserialize warning's namespace flipped lowercase exactly as coder v1 baseline predicted — shape unchanged.
after.notes: Coder v3 has no baseline-tests.md (only v1 does). For this version, v1's baseline is the correct comparison since it captures pre-vs-post the entire rename arc. Did not raise this as a finding.

sessions.intent: Security audit of app-lowercase: rename + 7 OBP merges, verify no trust-boundary regression.

before.assumptions: Branch is mechanical rename + folder merges; tester and codeanalyzer already PASSed.
before.risk: Possible drift: generator discovery strings, signing pipeline shape, .pr deserialization type names, settings folder merge moving secrets store.

actions.context: Security audit deliverables: plan, verdict, full report, summary.
Generator discovery namespace literals — all lowercased and pointing at new tree.
Crypto + envelope hardening — zip-bomb cap, rehydration depth cap, Signature.Hash internal-set, lazy-sign fail-closed — all preserved through rename.

after.notes: No security findings. 664-file rename + 7 OBP merges preserve all trust boundaries, hardening guards, and crypto-setter visibility. Branch ready for auditor.

sessions.intent: Final docs gate for app-lowercase: apply CLAUDE.md proposal, scrub leftover App.X drift in docstrings/per-folder CLAUDE.md, write verdict.

before.assumptions: Coder already swept Documentation/v0.2 + Runtime2/data-spec.md in commit a146aa9d9 and filed a CLAUDE.md proposal. Codeanalyzer flagged 8 stale App.X docstrings (R1) and asked to document the Default carve-out (S2). Tester + security + codeanalyzer all PASS.
before.risk: Per-folder CLAUDE.md files (/PLang/App/CLAUDE.md, /PLang.Tests/CLAUDE.md, /Tests/CLAUDE.md) and Documentation/Runtime2/plang_object_based_pattern.md may still carry stale PascalCase namespace references.

actions.context: Applied coder v3 proposal: 3 stale-line fixes + new Runtime2 Conventions bullet documenting lowercase rule, 7 merged engine concepts, property-name PascalCase carve-out, Default keyword carve-out, action renames.
Scrubbed R1+R2 docstring/comment drift: namespace-position App.X.Y -> app.x.y. Property-position references preserved.

Substantive rewrite: top-level tree remapped to reflect 7 OBP merges (Cache/Builder/Callback/Settings/Modules/Code/Debug now under app/modules/), all paths lowercased, added Case-Convention section, renamed module app -> environment, noted builder.app -> builder.load.
Path references lowercased; merged-folder paths remapped. Mid-session fix: todos.md:422 builder/providers/DefaultBuilderProvider.cs (bogus) -> builder/code/Default.cs (real).
Docs gate artefacts: plan, result detail, verdict, summary, and structured docs-report with proposal decisions and findings.

after.health: docs complete; CLAUDE.md proposal applied; all upstream verdicts PASS; build clean
after.notes: Branch ready to merge into runtime2. Non-blocking follow-ups: S1 (app/data/Code/ -> app/data/code/), S3 (deliberate naming pass for environment.run / builder.load).
